title: westringia rosmariniformis var. brevifolia
intro: Westringia rosmariniformis var. brevifolia, also known as the Westringia or Australian Rosemary, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Westringia rosmariniformis var. brevifolia include its potential to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin issues.
